conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child - my son Alex, he's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's helpful to know about Alex.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you a few more questions.

Do you live in the UK, or are you otherwise responsible for Alex (for example, paying towards the cost of supporting him at least the same rate as Child Benefit)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. Alex doesn't live with me though, he lives with his mum, but I do help out with his upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex lives with his mum rather than with you, this is an important factor in determining eligibility.

Can you tell me how much you contribute towards Alex's upkeep? Specifically, do you pay at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit rate (which is currently £25.60 per week for the eldest or only child) towards supporting him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute towards his upkeep. I help out with things like his clothes, school dinners, and bits and pieces he needs. I pay £26.05 a week towards supporting him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that detail. Since you're contributing at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit rate, that's helpful to know.

However, since Alex lives with his mother, I need to ask: is his mother currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or is she not claiming it?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she's not claiming it - nobody else is getting Child Benefit for him at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Is Alex under 16 years old (or under 20 and in approved education or training)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's 8 years old, so he's under 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just one final question to complete my assessment:

Are you responsible for Alex in any other way beyond the financial contribution - for example, do you have regular contact with him, or are you involved in decisions about his welfare and upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do have regular contact with him. I see him regularly and I'm involved in decisions about his schooling and his general welfare - we co-parent together even though he lives with his mum.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good news!

Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

✓ **Alex meets the age requirement** - He's 8 years old (under 16)
✓ **You live in the UK** - You're resident in Manchester
✓ **You contribute enough financially** - You pay £26.05 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which is more than the Child Benefit rate of £25.60 per week for an eldest or only child
✓ **You're responsible for Alex** - You have regular contact and are involved in co-parenting decisions
✓ **No one else is claiming** - Alex's mother is not currently claiming Child Benefit for him

Even though Alex doesn't live with you, UK rules allow you to claim Child Benefit when you contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit rate towards supporting your child.

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit for Alex. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which means he is under 16 and meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), which satisfies the residency requirement. Although Alex does not live with the claimant, the claimant contributes £26.05 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which exceeds the Child Benefit rate of £25.60 per week for the eldest or only child. The claimant also has regular contact with Alex and is involved in decisions about his welfare and upbringing, demonstrating responsibility for the child. Importantly, Alex's mother (who he lives with) is not currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and nobody else is claiming it. Under UK rules, when a child doesn't live with you, you can still claim Child Benefit if you contribute at least the Child Benefit rate towards the child's upkeep. Since all these conditions are met and no one else is claiming for Alex, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for his 8-year-old son Alex. Although Alex lives with his mother, the claimant contributes more than the Child Benefit rate towards Alex's upkeep (£26.05 per week), is actively involved in co-parenting, and importantly, no one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.
